question: Which phrase means 'to mock or ridicule someone'?
They _____ constantly injuring themselves during training. That's why they stopped.
Which adjective describes someone who 'has a naturally pleasing personality and is easy to like'?
Read: 'Going online can be seen as a way of bringing families together as well as a distraction.' What does 'a distraction' mean in this context?
choices: something that takes attention away from other things
a type of technology used in schools
a method of improving family bonds

question: When she was a child, she _____ spend her evenings reading by the fire.
Which phrase means 'to look down on someone with disrespect'?
The I-talk7, _____ has been completely redesigned, is the best phone on the market.
Which phrase means 'to speak angrily to someone about their bad behaviour'?
Read: 'Many people today feel that relationships between members of the same family are not as close as they were a few generations ago.' The writer uses this view to:
choices: prove that families are less close with statistics
introduce the general topic before presenting the thesis

question: In which type of relative clause can the relative pronoun be omitted if it is the object?
Which word means 'to move from one country or place to another'?
He didn't _____ to shout so much when he was younger.
Which linking phrase is used to ADD information in an opinion essay?
Rewrite: 'Technology is always changing. I find this exciting.' (Combine using relative clause)
choices: Technology is always changing, who I find exciting.
Technology is always changing, that I find exciting.
Technology is always changing, which I find exciting.
Technology is always changing, whose I find exciting.

question: Which phrase means 'to display great skill or wealth in order to impress people'?
She is a hard-working and _____ nurse who works at a children's hospital.
Read: 'But a man, Mr Brownlow, told the police officer that Oliver had not taken anything.' What does this tell us about Mr Brownlow?
choices: he was a caring and fair person who defended an innocent child
he was a police officer himself
he was one of Fagin's gang members

question: My sister _____ always be borrowing my clothes without permission. (annoying past habit)
Which adjective describes someone who 'behaves in a way that is not appropriate for their age'?
The app _____ you can keep in your pocket is very useful.
She _____ love painting when she was at school. (past state that is no longer true)
Which phrase means 'to see someone as a source of motivation and positive impact'?
Read: 'It is widely believed that technology has improved communication between family members.' Which phrase signals an impersonal view?
The school _____ I studied had excellent teachers.
Which phrase means 'to resolve a conflict and restore a relationship'?
Was the politician being _____ when he said the government would tackle climate change?
In 'On the whole, I don't like taking risks', the phrase 'on the whole' is used to:
choices: express what is usually or generally true

question: What does 'a workhouse' refer to in Oliver Twist?
choices: a type of school for poor children
a place where the poorest people could live, work and eat to survive

question: Rewrite using a participle clause: 'Did you see that car which was parked next to ours?'
choices: Did you see that car parking next to ours?
Did you see that car parked next to ours?
Did you see that car which parking next to ours?
Did you see that car to park next to ours?

question: Read: 'He was quite naughty and I used to mess about from time to time.' What does 'mess about' mean?
choices: to behave in a silly and irresponsible way

question: Which phrase means 'a conflict or physical fight'?
He _____ always be the last one to leave the party. (annoying repeated present habit)
Which phrase means 'to show appreciation by expressing something kind about someone'?
Read the opinion essay extract: 'In conclusion, I would say that families remain as close as they were.' What should the main body do in relation to this conclusion?
choices: introduce the topic for the first time
list facts from outside sources only
provide the supporting arguments and evidence
repeat the conclusion using different words

question: Which type of participle is used when the noun is the DOER of the action?
Which phrase means 'to react to something as if it is a bigger problem than it really is'?
She _____ always the peacemaker in their family arguments.
Which adjective describes someone who is 'very strongly committed to a belief or cause'?
The book _____ I was talking about is available in the library.
Which phrase means 'to deeply respect and greatly value someone as a role model'?
